sources.title: Lex Fridman Podcast #309 — Hank Green: Science, TikTok, Creator Economy
sources.description: 3+ hour deep dive covering science communication philosophy, creator economy evolution, social media dynamics, TikTok vs YouTube, internet culture, and personal philosophy.

sources.title: Hot Ones — Hank Green Needs Milk While Eating Spicy Wings
sources.description: Creator economy, VidCon origins, science communication philosophy, internet culture, being famous on the internet.

sources.title: TED Talk — The Rube Goldberg Machine of Internet Culture
sources.description: How internet culture creates unexpected connections and why the chaotic nature of online communities is a feature, not a bug.

sources.title: Vlogbrothers — I Have Cancer (Hank's diagnosis video)
sources.description: Hank's public announcement of Hodgkin's lymphoma diagnosis. Raw, honest, scientifically informed. Sets the tone for his entire cancer journey documentation.

sources.title: Vlogbrothers — I'm In Remission
sources.description: Announcement of cancer remission. Reflections on gratitude, mortality, the healthcare system, and what the experience changed.

sources.title: An Absolutely Remarkable Thing — novel and interviews
sources.description: Hank's first novel exploring fame, internet celebrity, collective action, technology, and what happens when an ordinary person becomes globally famous overnight.

sources.title: A Beautifully Foolish Endeavor — novel and interviews
sources.description: Sequel exploring the aftermath of fame, technology and power, attention economy, and whether collective human action can overcome individual ambition.

sources.title: Wired Interview — Hank Green on TikTok, Science, and Why He's Optimistic
sources.description: Hank's views on TikTok as a science communication platform, short-form vs long-form, algorithmic discovery, and defending optimism.

sources.title: SciShow — 600+ episodes of science communication
sources.description: Hank's flagship science communication channel. Covers biology, chemistry, physics, health, environment, technology. The format itself — accessible, enthusiastic, rigorous — embodies his communication philosophy.

sources.title: Crash Course — educational video series
sources.description: Full-course educational videos co-created with John. Hank hosts science courses. 17M+ subscribers. Proof that deep education works at YouTube scale.

sources.title: Hank Green TikTok — science and culture commentary
sources.description: 7M+ followers. Short-form science explainers, healthcare system commentary, creator economy observations, and cultural commentary. The distilled version of Hank's worldview.

sources.title: NYT Interview — Hank Green on Cancer, Mortality, and What Matters
sources.description: Post-diagnosis interview covering how cancer changed his perspective on work, time, relationships, and the healthcare system.

sources.title: The Verge — Interview on Creator Economy and Platform Power
sources.description: Deep discussion of platform dynamics, creator exploitation, VidCon's legacy, and why creators need to own their businesses.

sources.title: Dear Hank & John Podcast — selected episodes on life philosophy
sources.description: Comedy/advice podcast with John Green. Rich source of Hank's unscripted views on life, science, relationships, and the absurdity of existence.

sources.title: VidCon Keynotes and Panels — creator economy evolution
sources.description: As VidCon co-founder, Hank's keynotes trace the evolution of the creator economy from scrappy YouTube community to multi-billion dollar industry.

sources.title: Climate and Environmental Science Communication
sources.description: Hank's environmental science background informs his climate communication. SciShow climate episodes, Vlogbrothers commentary, and public advocacy for climate action through technology and policy.
